study_group_representative.quote: From Jan 14 to Feb 12, 2020, the Shenzhen Center for Disease Control and Prevention identified 391 SARS-CoV-2 cases and 1286 close contacts.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: From Jan 14 to Feb 12, 2020, the Shenzhen Center for Disease Control and Prevention identified 391 SARS-CoV-2 cases and 1286 close contacts. We compared cases identified through symptomatic surveillance and contact tracing, and estimated the time from symptom onset to confirmation, isolation, and admission to hospital.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: At the time of first clinical assessment, most cases were mild (102 [26%] of 391) or moderate (254 [65%] of 391), and only 35 (9%) were severe. ... Overall, 1286 close contacts were identified ... 98 of the close contacts tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 infection by RT-PCR.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: As of Feb 22, 2020, three cases had died and 225 had recovered (median time to recovery 21 days; 95% CI 20–22). ... 622 (95%) of 653 close contacts with known dates for the period when they were under quarantine were followed up for 12 days or longer.

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: The full text does not provide information on whether outcome assessors were blinded to the investigated determinant (mode of surveillance).

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: Suspected cases and close contacts were tested for SARS-CoV-2 by RT-PCR of nasal swabs ... cases were classified as having severe symptoms if they had any of the following: breathing rate 30 breaths per min or higher; oxygen saturation level 93% or lower at rest; oxygen concentration level PaO2/FiO2 ... 300 mm Hg ... or lower; lung infiltrates higher than 50% within 24–48 h; respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation; septic shock; or multiple organ dysfunction or failure.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: In multiple logistic regression, male sex was associated with severe symptoms (odds ratio [OR] 2·5 [95% CI 1·1–6·1]). The probability of severe symptoms increased slightly with age, although only individuals aged 60–69 years had a significantly increased risk compared with the reference category, individuals aged 50–59 years (OR 3·4 [95% 1·4–9·5]).

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: The household secondary attack rate was 11·2% (95% CI 9·1–13·8), and children were as likely to be infected as adults (infection rate 7·4% in children <10 years vs population average of 6·6%). The observed reproductive number (R) was 0·4 (95% CI 0·3–0·5), with a mean serial interval of 6·3 days (95% CI 5·2–7·6).
